# Resource Management Plan

| Project name | [Project Name] |
| Project ID | [Project ID/Code] |
| Version | [e.g., 1.0] |

| Prepared by | [Name, Role] |

| Last updated | [YYYY-MM-DD] |

## Purpose
Define how resources are planned, acquired, developed, allocated, monitored, and released across the
project lifecycle.

## Audience
Resource Manager, Project Manager, Delivery Leads, HR Partners, Finance.

## Scope
This plan covers:
- Human resources (team members, contractors, consultants).
- Physical resources (equipment, facilities, materials).
- Technology resources (software, hardware, licenses, environments).
- Financial resources (budget allocation and cost controls).

## Inputs
- Schedule baseline (`docs/templates/schedule/schedule-baseline-waterfall.xlsx`).
- Resource assignment matrix (`docs/templates/resources/resource-assignment-matrix-cross.csv`).
- Risk register and cost baseline (as applicable).

## 1. Resource management objectives
- Ensure optimal resource allocation and utilization.
- Minimize conflicts and bottlenecks.
- Maintain availability throughout project phases.
- Control resource costs within approved budgets.
- Develop team capabilities and competencies.

## 2. Resource management approach
Describe how resources will be planned and managed end-to-end.

### 2.1 Resource lifecycle
- **Plan**: Identify resource requirements, timing, and costs.
- **Acquire**: Secure internal or external resources.
- **Develop**: Train and onboard resources.
- **Manage**: Track utilization, resolve conflicts, manage changes.
- **Release**: Transition and release resources with knowledge transfer.

### 2.2 Resource categories
| Category | Description | Examples |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Human | People required to deliver work | PM, Tech Lead, QA, BA |
| Physical | Hardware, facilities, consumables | Devices, labs, supplies |
| Technology | Software, licenses, environments | CI/CD, cloud services |
| Financial | Budget and cost allocation | Labor, tooling, training |

## 3. Resource breakdown structure (RBS)
Provide a high-level organizational chart or RBS for the project.

```
Project Sponsor
  Project Manager
    Technical Lead
      Delivery Team
    Business Analyst
    QA Lead
```

| Technical Lead | Architecture, technical decisions |  |  |
| Business Analyst | Requirements and stakeholder liaison |  |  |
| QA Lead | Quality strategy and testing oversight |  |  |

## Completion checklist
- [ ] Resource roles, skills, and allocation needs defined.
- [ ] Acquisition strategy and training plan agreed.
- [ ] Monitoring cadence established.
- [ ] Release plan and knowledge transfer steps documented.

## Acceptance criteria
- Resource plan supports delivery milestones and quality requirements.
- Staffing gaps and mitigation actions are documented.
- Cost controls and reporting cadence are approved.
